revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Aguchi-jinja]][[ja:開口神社]] {{Expand Japanese|開口神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Aguchi Shrine | native_name = 開口神社 | image = Aguchi-jinja haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Aguchi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.576842|135.474421}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi|lt=Shiotsuchi|ja|シオツチオジ|zh|鹽土老翁|qid=Q11174872|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji}} }} {{nihongo|'''Aguchi Shrine'''|開口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11417778|ltq=Q11417778|qid=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 20 ** P155: {{ill|Sakanouheno Shrine|lt=Sakanouheno Shrine|qid=Q135039170|1=Sakanouheno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11537864|ltq=Q11537864|qid=Q11537864|1=Sakurai Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Sakai|lt=Sakai|ar|ساكاي|arz|ساكاى (تجمع سكان فى محافظه اوساكا)|ban|Sakai, Osaka|be|Сакаі (Осака)|bg|Сакаи|br|Sakai|ca|Sakai (Osaka)|ceb|Sakai Shi|ce|Сакаи (Осака)|cs|Sakai (Ósaka)|cy|Sakai|da|Sakai|de|Sakai|en|Sakai|envoyage|Sakai (Osaka)|eo|Sakaj|es|Sakai (Osaka)|et|Sakai|eu|Sakai (Osaka)|fa|ساکای، اوساکا|fi|Sakai|fr|Sakai|ga|Sakai|gl|Sakai, Osaka|he|סאקאי|hu|Szakai (Oszaka)|hy|Սակաի|id|Sakai, Osaka|it|Sakai (Osaka)|ja|堺市|javoyage|堺市|ki|Sakai|ko|사카이시 (오사카부)|la|Sacaium|lt|Sakajus|mg|Sakai|mn|Сакай|ms|Sakai|nl|Sakai (Osaka)|no|Sakai|os|Сакаи (Осакæ)|pl|Sakai (Osaka)|plvoyage|Sakai|pt|Sakai (Osaka)|ro|Sakai, Osaka|ru|Сакаи (Осака)|sah|Сакаи|sco|Sakai|sh|Sakai, Osaka|simple|Sakai, Osaka|sk|Sakai (Osaka)|sr|Сакај|su|Sakai, Osaka|sv|Sakai|tg|Сакаи|th|ซาไก (เมือง)|tl|Sakai, Osaka|tr|Sakai|tt|Сакаи (Осака)|tum|Sakai|uk|Сакай (Осака)|ur|ساکائی، اوساکا|uz|Sakai|vi|Sakai|war|Sakai|wuu|堺市|zh_classical|堺市|zh_min_nan|Sakai-chhī (Tāi-pán)|zh_yue|堺市|zh|堺市|zhvoyage|堺市|qid=Q193428|1=Sakai}} * {{ill|Izumi Province|lt=Izumi Province|ca|Província d'Izumi|ckb|پارێزگای ئیزومی|cs|Provincie Izumi|de|Provinz Izumi|en|Izumi Province|es|Provincia de Izumi|fa|ولایت ایزومی|fr|Province d'Izumi|gl|Provincia de Izumi|hak|Izumi-koet|id|Provinsi Izumi|it|Izumi (provincia)|ja|和泉国|ko|이즈미국|lt|Idzumio žemė|nl|Izumi (provincie)|pt|Província de Izumi|ru|Идзуми (провинция)|simple|Izumi Province|sk|Izumi (provincia)|sr|Провинција Изуми|tr|Izumi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ідзумі|zh_min_nan|Izumi-kok|zh_yue|和泉國|zh|和泉國|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}} * {{ill|Q3270651|ltq=Q3270651|qid=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.576842|135.474421}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Aguchi-jinja haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Shiotsuchi|lt=Shiotsuchi|ja|シオツチオジ|zh|鹽土老翁|qid=Q11174872|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji}} == CiNii Research ID (P11496) == * 1140845216711399808 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あぐちじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 421146998506918941832 == NACSIS-CAT author ID (P271) == * DA0708584XCiNii Research == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4120105000763 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00631216 == Commons category (P373) == * Aguchi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府堺市堺区甲斐町東2丁目1番29号', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 開口神社(大寺) {{commons category|Category:Aguchi-jinja}} [[JA:開口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11655187}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Aguchi Shrine | Image = [[Image:Aguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q193428|qid=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Sakai Ward|Q1042008|qid=Q1042008|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}} 2-1-29 Kaicho-higashi | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|36.7|N|135|28|28.5|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|UNKNOWN|ltja=塩土老翁神|qid=Q11174872|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=素盞嗚神|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|qid=Q11574477|lt=生国魂神|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Other names = Mitsujōzan Nenbutsu-ji
{{ill|lt=Ōtera|Q11434514|qid=Q11434514|1=Ōtera}} | Annual festival = Friday to {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|qid=Q132|1=Sunday}} closest to {{ill|lt=September 12|Q2849|qid=Q2849|1=September 12}} (Hassaku Festival) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Aguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Mitsu nasu tomoe (crest of Aguchi Shrine in Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture).png|thumb|160px|The crest of Aguchi Shrine.]] **Aguchi Shrine** (あぐちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q193428|qid=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Sakai Ward|Q1042008|qid=Q1042008|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}. It is commonly known as “**Ōtera**” (おおてら) and affectionately called “Ōtera-san” by locals. It is the tutelary shrine of Sakai Minami-gumi (south of {{ill|lt=Ōshōji|Q11434534|qid=Q11434534|1=Oshoji}}). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. As Sakai is also the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Yosano Akiko|Q464104|qid=Q464104|1=Yosano Akiko}}, the shrine offers a special “Akiko Love Poem Omikuji.”{{Cite web |title=Aguchi Shrine wedding and Shinto ceremony in-depth report! What is its reputation? {{!}} Spicomi |url=https://spicomi.net/media/articles/2906 |website=Spicomi |access-date=2023-11-27}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it is said to have been founded when, on her return from the {{ill|lt=Three Kingdoms expedition|Q11249435|qid=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, an imperial command was given to enshrine Shiotsuchi-no-Oji at this site. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the 24 shrines of {{ill|lt=Ōtori District|Q3270651|qid=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}} in {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}. In this text, “Aguchi” is read as “Akikuchi,” which later became corrupted into “Aguchi.” The shrine has long had close ties with {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|qid=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}) of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, and records from {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=731|Q23566|qid=Q23566|1=731}}) refer to it as “Akikuchi Minato-hime Shrine.” In {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 16 ({{ill|lt=744|Q23608|qid=Q23608|1=744}}), {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|qid=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} founded the temple **Nenbutsu-ji** on the shrine grounds, enshrining {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|qid=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} as its principal image. As syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism progressed, the temple became the shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. During the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, under the influence of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}, Nenbutsu-ji became a {{ill|lt=Shingon|Q550182|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple. It also had connections with {{ill|lt=Kūya|Q2975265|qid=Q2975265|1=Kūya}}, serving as a center for both {{ill|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|Q336102|qid=Q336102|1=Mikkyō}} and {{ill|lt=Pure Land Buddhism|Q262244|qid=Q262244|1=Pure Land Buddhism}}, and came to be popularly known as “Ōtera,” a name that became firmly established. In {{ill|lt=Jōhei|Q1324874|qid=Q1324874|1=Jōhei}} 2 ({{ill|lt=932|Q23656|qid=Q23656|1=932}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was elevated to {{ill|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11123280|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}}. From ancient through medieval times, the area around the shrine was known as Akikuchi Village. In {{ill|lt=Tennei|Q1193915|qid=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1113|Q19531|qid=Q19531|1=1113}}), the {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=素盞嗚神|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} of Haramura and the {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|qid=Q11574477|lt=生国魂神|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} of Kidomura were {{ill|lt=enshrined together|Q11412524|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}, and the shrine came to be revered as “Akikuchi Three-Village Daimyōjin.” Both the shrine and Nenbutsu-ji were under the control of the {{ill|lt=Tsumori clan|Q11555993|qid=Q11555993|1=Tsumori clan}}, but as Sakai developed into a commercial port and {{ill|lt=merchants|Q215536|qid=Q215536|1=Merchant}} rose in power, they came to be supported by merchant self-governance. During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, in {{ill|lt=Kōō|Q1146172|qid=Q1146172|1=Kōō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1389|Q6371|qid=Q6371|1=1389}}), the wealthy merchant {{ill|lt=Notoya Shūami|Q11634379|qid=Q11634379|1=gōshō}} donated farmland, after which land donations followed in succession. In {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 32 ({{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|qid=Q6283|1=1425}}), the shrine became a {{ill|lt=prayer site|Q11588473|qid=Q11588473|1=Kigan-sho}} of the {{ill|lt=Imperial Court|Q30253177|qid=Q30253177|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, and in Ōei 34 ({{ill|lt=1427|Q6305|qid=Q6305|1=1427}}) it also became a prayer site of the {{ill|lt=Muromachi shogunate|Q1154406|qid=Q1154406|1=Ashikaga shogunate}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenbun|Q1192853|qid=Q1192853|1=Tenbun}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1535|Q6453|qid=Q6453|1=1535}}), more than 110 wealthy merchants of Sakai Minami-shō each donated one kanmon of coins for repairs to Nenbutsu-ji’s earthen walls. Among them were famous figures such as tea masters {{ill|lt=Takeno Jōō|Q7678036|qid=Q7678036|1=Takeno Jō'ō}} and {{ill|lt=Sen no Rikyū|Q305477|qid=Q305477|1=Sen no Rikyū}} (under the name “Sen Yoshiro”), as well as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uoya Yajirō|1=UNKNOWN}}, whose name also appears on stone lanterns at {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|qid=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} ({{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|qid=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}) in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|qid=Q11444975|1=Nara}}. From these wealthy merchants, a dozen or so were chosen as warehouse officials and council members who administered Sakai’s autonomous organization and held important roles in Aguchi Shrine’s festivals. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, the temple was abolished under the {{ill|lt=Shinto-Buddhist separation|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} policy, but the {{ill|lt=three-story pagoda|Q11357687|qid=Q11357687|1=Three-storied pagoda}} built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|qid=Q7577|1=1663}}) was left standing. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|qid=Q2043|1=1902}} (Meiji 35) it was promoted to Prefectural Shrine. On July 10, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|qid=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), during the sixth {{ill|lt=Osaka Air Raid|Q2814395|qid=Q2814395|1=Bombing of Osaka}} (the Sakai air raid), the main hall and pagoda were completely destroyed by fire,[http://www.aguchi.jp/aguchijinjya3.html Aguchi Shrine: Rituals and History] though valuable documents escaped damage. The current shrine buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|qid=Q3696|1=1961}} (Shōwa 36). To the south is Shukuin, which serves as a resting place for the portable shrine during the Sumiyoshi Grand Festival. === Enshrined Deities === * Principal deities – {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|UNKNOWN|ltja=塩土老翁神|qid=Q11174872|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=素盞嗚神|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|qid=Q11574477|lt=生国魂神|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} Shiotsuchi-no-Oji is said to embody the combined divine virtues of the {{ill|lt=Three Sumiyoshi Deities|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}, and the shrine is known as the “inner sanctuary of Sumiyoshi.” === Shrine Grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|qid=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36). * Worship hall – Rebuilt in 1961 (Shōwa 36). * Shrine office * Toyotake {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * Sanpō {{ill|lt=Kōjin|Q6455190|qid=Q6455190|1=Kōjin}} Shrine (Kamado Shrine) * Shirahige Shrine and Sae Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kōshin faith|Q3082157|qid=Q3082157|1=Kōshin}}) * Hedomatsu Shrine and Matsukaze Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara Shrine}} and Iwamuro Shrine * Yakushi Shrine – Enshrines the {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|qid=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} that was once the principal image of Nenbutsu-ji. * Right auxiliary shrine – {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|qid=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|qid=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}}, Kabuto Shrine, Miyake {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|qid=Q11390939}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|qid=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke Shrine|Q17315011|qid=Q17315011|1=Toyouke Shrine}} * Left auxiliary shrine – Ubumusubi Shrine, Hokushin Shrine, Ōkunitama Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona Shrine|Q11464750|qid=Q11464750|1=Sukunahikona Shrine}}, Funatama Shrine, Kusumoto Shrine * Yōkō Stone * Ōgi Mound * Hakuichiryū Shrine * Kinryū Well – After {{ill|lt=Kaie-ji (Sakai City)|Q97277064|qid=Q97277064|1=Kaie-ji (Sakai City)}} was relocated to the grounds of {{ill|lt=Nanshū-ji|Q11407716|qid=Q11407716|1=Nanshū-ji}}, this became the shrine’s well. * Monument marking the death of {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Motonaga|Q10865728|qid=Q10865728|1=Miyoshi Motonaga}} – {{ill|lt=Honpō-ji (Sakai City)|Q11665118|qid=Q11665118|1=Kenpon-ji Temple}}, where he committed suicide during the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenbun Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}, once stood nearby. === Cultural Properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated “Ōtera Engi” in color on paper, 3 scrolls, by {{ill|lt=Tosa Mitsuoki|Q3180225|qid=Q3180225|1=Tosa Mitsuoki}} (with one author’s catalogue scroll, one lacquered box, and three former roller rods)The lacquered box and former roller rods were additionally designated in {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|qid=Q25291|1=2018}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 30)., deposited at the {{ill|lt=Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts|Q9004920|qid=Q9004920|1=Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts}}. * Tantō dagger, signed Yoshimitsu, hira-zukuri, three-ridged mune, itame forging, suguha hamon, length 24.1 cm, by {{ill|lt=Awataguchi Yoshimitsu|Q11604625|qid=Q11604625|1=Awataguchi Yoshimitsu}}, deposited at {{ill|lt=Osaka Castle Tenshukaku|Q96338884|qid=Q96338884|1=Osaka Castle Museum}}; shrine tradition says it was donated by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Yoshitane|Q708527|qid=Q708527|1=Ashikaga Yoshitane}}.Edited and published by Sakai City Cultural Properties Division, *Cultural Properties of Sakai* (Revised and Expanded Edition), 2009, p.51. * Ink-on-paper imperial autograph waka collection by {{ill|lt=Emperor Fushimi|Q373949|qid=Q373949|1=Emperor Fushimi}}, 30.8 × 551.0 cm, deposited at the Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts – A draft of a waka anthology written when the emperor was around 40 years old. Of its six sections (spring, summer, autumn, winter, love, miscellaneous), only the winter section survives in complete form, making it especially valuable.*Cultural Properties of Sakai* (Revised and Expanded Edition), 2009, p.58. === Osaka Prefecture–Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Aguchi Shrine Documents (20 scrolls, 18 hanging scrolls, 4 books, 1 booklet, 239 sheets), distributed and deposited at the {{ill|lt=Osaka Museum of History|Q11261312|qid=Q11261312|1=Osaka Museum of History}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Castle Tenshukaku|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Sakai City Museum|Q1448918|qid=Q1448918|1=Sakai City Museum}} – Ancient documents related to Aguchi Shrine and its temple Nenbutsu-ji from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, valuable not only for the shrine but also for understanding Sakai’s history.*Cultural Properties of Sakai* (Revised and Expanded Edition), 2009, p.59. === Sakai City–Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statue of Yakushi Nyorai === Festivals === * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * Tondo Festival – January 15 * Setsubun Festival – Early February * Hatsuuma Festival – Late March (Day of the Horse) * Satsuki Festival – May 5 * Great Purification, Chinowa Passing, and Yabusame Ritual – June 30 * Hassaku Festival (main festival) – Mid-September (features Sakai’s oldest {{ill|lt=futon taiko|Q11277203|qid=Q11277203|1=Futon Daiko}}) * Sanpō Kōjin Festival – November 28 === Access === * {{ill|lt=Hankai Electric Tramway|Q2069534|qid=Q2069534|1=Hankai Tramway}} {{ill|lt=Hankai Line|Q5648522|qid=Q5648522|1=Hankai Line}} – {{ill|lt=Ōshōji Stop|Q11434538|qid=Q11434538|1=Ōshōji Station}} * Hankai Electric Tramway {{ill|lt=Hankai Line|Q5648522|qid=Q5648522|1=Hankai Line}} – {{ill|lt=Shukuin Stop|Q11455658|qid=Q11455658|1=Shukuin Station}} === Nearby Information === * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine (Sakai City)|Q11619211|qid=Q11619211|1=Sugawara Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Shukuin Tongū|Q11455657|qid=Q11455657|1=Shukuin Tongū}} * Sakai Yamanokuchi Shopping Street === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * *Visiting Historic Sites of Osaka 2: Medieval Edition* (Number Publishing), Chapter 38: “Aguchi Shrine, Guardian of the Autonomous City of Sakai” * Edited and published by {{ill|lt=Sakai City Museum|Q1448918|qid=Q1448918|1=Sakai City Museum}}, *Aguchi Shrine and Sakai* – {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|qid=Q1990|1=2012}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 24), February 11 * Edited and published by Sakai City Museum, *Ōtera-san: Tracing the Forms of Faith* – {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|qid=Q25245|1=2016}} (Heisei 28), September 13 === External Links ===
